rationale: The citing note says the paper positions itself against prior paid-search work focused on keyword/position effects, while the cited paper is exactly a keyword-level paid-search spillover model (generic vs. branded) with Bayesian dynamics. That makes the cited work a plausible foundational/comparison reference in the citing paper’s literature framing rather than an obviously incidental mention. With this domain and conceptual overlap, it should be retained for second-stage relation judgment.
